title: Tomas and the Crystal Compass
synopsis: When nine-year-old Tomas discovers a magical compass in his grandmother's attic, he's transported to 1492 Spain where he meets Esperanza, a brave girl whose family faces danger during the Spanish Inquisition. Together with Miguel, a young mapmaker's apprentice, they must use the compass's power to help people escape to safety while learning about courage, friendship, and the importance of helping others.

chapters.title: The Attic Discovery
chapters.content: Tomas pushed aside dusty boxes in his grandmother's attic. "There's so much old stuff up here," he muttered, sneezing from all the dust. Abuela had asked him to look for her sewing basket, but all he found were cobwebs and mysterious objects covered in white sheets. A shaft of golden sunlight streamed through the small window, making the dust dance like tiny fairies. That's when something sparkled in the corner. Behind an old trunk, Tomas spotted a wooden box carved with strange symbols. His fingers traced the smooth wood as he carefully lifted the lid. Inside, nestled in faded blue velvet, lay the most beautiful compass he'd ever seen. The brass gleamed like it was brand new, and the crystal face seemed to glow with its own light. "Whoa," Tomas whispered. The needle spun wildly, pointing in directions that didn't seem possible. As he picked up the compass, warmth spread through his hands. The world around him began to shimmer and blur. "What's happening?" he gasped, but his voice sounded far away. The attic walls faded, replaced by swirling colors and a strange humming sound. When everything finally stopped spinning, Tomas found himself standing on a cobblestone street he'd never seen before.

illustrations.description: A young Latino boy with dark hair and curious brown eyes crouches in a dusty attic filled with old trunks and mysterious objects. Golden sunlight streams through a small window, illuminating floating dust particles. The boy holds an ornate brass compass that glows with magical crystal light.
illustrations.alt: Tomas discovering the magical compass in the attic

chapters.title: A Strange New World
chapters.content: The street was nothing like Tomas's neighborhood. Stone buildings rose high on both sides, their windows small and dark. People walked by wearing long robes and strange hats. A woman carried a basket of bread on her head, and a man led a donkey pulling a wooden cart. "This can't be real," Tomas said, looking down at his modern clothes. His sneakers looked very out of place on the old cobblestones. A girl about his age rushed past, nearly bumping into him. She had long dark hair and wore a simple brown dress. "Sorry!" she called over her shoulder, but something in her voice sounded scared. Tomas followed her with his eyes and noticed men in black robes walking behind her. They looked mean, and the girl kept looking back nervously. Without thinking, Tomas stepped into an alley and called softly, "Hey! Are you okay?" The girl stopped and stared at him with wide brown eyes. "Who are you?" she whispered. "And why are you dressed so strangely?" "I'm Tomas," he said. "I don't know how I got here. Where am I?" "You're in Granada," she said, still looking confused. "It's 1492. I'm Esperanza." She glanced nervously at the street. "Those men are looking for my family. We have to hide." Tomas felt the compass grow warm in his pocket. Somehow, he knew he was supposed to help.

illustrations.description: A medieval Spanish street with tall stone buildings and narrow cobblestones. A young Latino boy in modern clothes stands looking confused while a girl in a brown medieval dress hurries past. Men in dark robes walk in the background. Warm golden light filters between the buildings.
illustrations.alt: Tomas on a medieval street meeting Esperanza

chapters.title: Meeting Esperanza
chapters.content: Esperanza pulled Tomas deeper into the alley. "We can't stay here," she whispered urgently. "Those men work for the Inquisition. They're looking for people like my family." "What's the Inquisition?" Tomas asked. Esperanza looked surprised. "You really don't know? They're trying to force everyone to follow the same religion. If you don't, they take you away." Her voice shook a little. Tomas felt angry. "That's not fair! People should be able to believe what they want." "I know," Esperanza said sadly. "My grandmother says we used to live peacefully with Christians and Muslims. Now everything is different." They crept to the end of the alley and peeked out. The men in black were asking people questions, showing them a piece of paper. "Is that a picture of your family?" Tomas asked. Esperanza nodded. "We need to warn my parents. But I don't know how to get home without those men seeing me." Tomas felt the compass pulse in his pocket again. He pulled it out, and Esperanza gasped. "It's beautiful! What is it?" "I think it's magic," Tomas said. The needle was pointing down a different street. "Maybe it can help us." "Magic isn't real," Esperanza said, but her eyes were fixed on the glowing crystal. "Maybe not in my time," Tomas said. "But this place feels different. Will you trust me?" Esperanza looked at him for a long moment, then nodded. "Okay. But if we get caught, I'm blaming you."

illustrations.description: Two children hide in a narrow stone alley with vines growing on the walls. The girl has long dark hair and wears medieval clothing, while the boy holds a glowing compass. In the background, shadowy figures in dark robes search the street. The scene has tense, shadowy lighting with golden highlights.
illustrations.alt: Tomas and Esperanza hiding in the alley

chapters.title: The Young Mapmaker
chapters.content: Following the compass's needle, Tomas and Esperanza wound through twisting streets. The compass led them to a small shop with maps hanging in the window. "A cartographer's shop," Esperanza said. "My father knows the man who works here." They pushed open the heavy wooden door, and a bell chimed. Inside, a boy a little older than them looked up from a large table covered with papers. He had curly red hair and ink stains on his fingers. "Esperanza!" he said happily, then his face grew serious. "You shouldn't be here. There are Inquisition guards everywhere." "I know, Miguel," she said. "This is my new friend, Tomas. He's... different." Miguel stared at Tomas's clothes. "He's definitely not from around here. Are those shoes made of canvas?" "Something like that," Tomas said. He showed Miguel the compass. "We're trying to help Esperanza's family." Miguel's eyes widened when he saw the compass. "I've never seen anything like it. The crystal face seems to glow." He looked at the maps on his table, then at the compass needle. "It's pointing toward the mountains. There are hidden paths there that the guards don't know about." "Hidden paths?" Esperanza asked hopefully. "My master has been making secret maps," Miguel whispered. "Maps that show safe routes for people who need to leave the city." He rolled up one of the papers. "I can take you to your parents, Esperanza. And maybe this magical compass can help us save others too."

illustrations.description: Inside a medieval mapmaker's shop filled with rolled parchments and navigation tools. A redheaded boy with ink-stained fingers shows maps to two other children. Warm candlelight illuminates the detailed maps and instruments scattered across wooden tables. The magical compass glows softly on the table.
illustrations.alt: Miguel the mapmaker showing maps to Tomas and Esperanza

chapters.title: The Dangerous Journey
chapters.content: The three friends slipped out the back door of the map shop. Miguel led them through narrow passages between buildings that Tomas never would have noticed. "I know all the secret ways," Miguel whispered proudly. "My master taught me to observe everything." The compass continued to pulse warmly in Tomas's hand, its needle pointing steadily toward the mountains. But as they moved through the city, Tomas noticed something troubling. The needle sometimes spun toward people in the streets—people who looked scared or were hiding. "I think the compass finds people who need help," he told his friends. They paused near a fountain where a woman sat crying softly. Her clothes were torn, and she clutched a small bundle. "My children," she whispered to herself. "Where are my children?" Esperanza looked at Tomas. "The compass is pointing right at her." Miguel frowned. "If we stop to help everyone, we might not be able to save anyone. The guards could catch us." Tomas felt torn. He wanted to help the woman, but he also wanted to keep his new friends safe. "What if we tell her about the mountain paths?" he suggested. "She could come with us." "That's dangerous," Miguel said. "We don't know if we can trust her." "She needs help," Esperanza said firmly. "That's enough for me." The three friends looked at each other. They all knew this was an important choice.

illustrations.description: Three children move carefully through shadowy medieval alleyways. A sad woman sits by an old stone fountain in the background, holding a small bundle. The magical compass glows in the boy's hand, pointing toward the woman. The scene has dramatic lighting with deep shadows and golden highlights suggesting both danger and hope.
illustrations.alt: The friends discovering someone who needs help

chapters.title: Helping Others
chapters.content: Tomas approached the woman carefully. "Excuse me," he said gently. "Are you okay?" The woman looked up with red, swollen eyes. "They took my children," she sobbed. "The guards said I had to choose between my faith and my family. How can anyone make such a choice?" Esperanza knelt beside her. "What are your children's names?" "Elena and Pablo," the woman said. "Elena is seven, and Pablo is only five." Tomas felt the compass grow hot. The needle spun wildly, then pointed toward a large building with barred windows. "I think I know where they are," he said quietly. Miguel shook his head. "That's the Inquisition headquarters. We can't go there. It's too dangerous." "We can't just leave them," Esperanza said. "What if they were my little brothers?" The woman, who had told them her name was Carmen, looked at the strange glowing compass with amazement. "Is that some kind of holy relic?" "Something like that," Tomas said. He had an idea. "Miguel, do your secret maps show all the buildings in the city?" "Of course," Miguel said proudly. "Even the underground passages." "Underground passages?" Tomas asked excitedly. Miguel grinned. "Granada is full of old tunnels. Some were built by the Muslims who used to rule here. Others are just old sewers and storage cellars. I know them all." "Could we use them to get close to that building?" "Maybe," Miguel said slowly. "But it would still be very dangerous." Carmen grabbed Tomas's hands. "Please," she whispered. "They're all I have left."

illustrations.description: A woman with tear-stained cheeks kneels beside three children near a stone fountain. The boy holds a glowing compass that points toward a forbidding building with barred windows in the distance. The scene shows both desperation and determination, with warm light on the group contrasting with the dark, imposing building.
illustrations.alt: Carmen pleading for help to find her children

chapters.title: The Underground Adventure
chapters.content: Miguel led them to a hidden entrance behind the baker's shop. "This tunnel connects to the old aqueduct system," he whispered, lifting a heavy stone cover. "It's dark and smelly, but it goes right under the Inquisition building." They climbed down into the damp tunnel. Tomas wrinkled his nose. "Ew. It smells like rotten eggs down here." "That's just sulfur from the old springs," Miguel said cheerfully. "You get used to it." The compass's glow provided some light in the darkness, but it wasn't much. They had to feel their way along the slimy walls. "I heard voices," Esperanza whispered suddenly. Everyone froze. Through the stone walls above them came muffled sounds—adult voices and, faintly, children crying. "Elena? Pablo?" Carmen called softly. "Mama?" came a small voice. "Is that you?" Carmen started crying again, but this time with relief. "I'm here, my darlings! I'm going to get you out!" Tomas studied his compass. The needle pointed straight up. "They're right above us. But how do we get up there?" Miguel ran his hands along the tunnel walls. "There should be a maintenance shaft somewhere around here. The old builders always included them." His fingers found something different in the stone. "Here! This stone is loose." Working together, they managed to move several stones, creating a small opening. "I'm the smallest," Esperanza said. "I should go first." "No," Tomas said. "It was my idea. I'll go." But when he tried to squeeze through the opening, he got stuck. "Okay," he said sheepishly. "Maybe Esperanza should go first."

illustrations.description: Four people crawl through a dark, ancient stone tunnel lit only by the magical compass's blue-green glow. Water drips from the ceiling and moss grows on the damp walls. The children's faces show determination despite their fear, while shadows dance on the curved stone walls around them.
illustrations.alt: The group moving through the underground tunnels

chapters.title: The Daring Rescue
chapters.content: Esperanza squeezed through the opening and found herself in a storage room filled with barrels and sacks. She could hear the children more clearly now. "Elena? Pablo?" she whispered. "Over here!" came Elena's voice. Esperanza found them locked in a small room behind a heavy wooden door. The key hung on a hook just out of reach. "I'm going to get you out," she promised. She stacked some sacks on top of a barrel and climbed up carefully. Just as her fingers touched the key, she heard footsteps in the hallway. "Someone's coming!" she whispered down to the others. Tomas felt the compass pulse urgently. An idea popped into his head. "Miguel, do you have any of your ink with you?" "Yes, but why—" "Trust me!" Tomas grabbed the ink bottle and some paper from Miguel's bag. He quickly wrote a note: "The prisoners have escaped through the south gate. All guards report there immediately." He pushed the note up through the hole to Esperanza. "Slide this under the door where the guards will see it!" Esperanza understood immediately. She slipped the fake message under the door, then grabbed the key. Within minutes, they heard running footsteps as the guards rushed away. "Now!" Tomas whispered urgently. Esperanza unlocked the door, and two small children tumbled out. Elena had her mother's dark eyes, and little Pablo clutched a wooden toy horse. "Mama!" they cried when they saw Carmen through the hole. Getting everyone back down into the tunnel was tricky, but they managed it. "We did it!" Miguel said, grinning. But Tomas's compass was still spinning wildly. "There are others who need help too," he realized.

illustrations.description: A girl carefully climbs on stacked sacks to reach keys hanging on a wall hook in a dim stone storage room. Two small children wait behind wooden bars nearby. Candlelight flickers from the hallway, creating dramatic shadows. The scene shows tension and hope as the rescue unfolds.
illustrations.alt: Esperanza rescuing Elena and Pablo from their prison

chapters.title: The Journey Home
chapters.content: Using Miguel's secret maps and the compass's magical guidance, they led Carmen and her children to safety in the mountains. Other families had joined them along the way—people the compass had pointed them toward. There was David the blacksmith with his elderly father, Maria the weaver with her three daughters, and Ahmad the scholar who carried precious books in a leather satchel. "How did you know to find us all?" David asked Tomas as they climbed the mountain path. Tomas looked at his compass, which was finally pointing steadily in one direction. "I had help," he said simply. As the sun began to set, they reached a hidden valley where other refugees had made a temporary camp. "From here, you can follow the paths to the coast," Miguel explained. "There are ships that will take you to safer places." "We can never thank you enough," Carmen said, hugging all three friends. Elena and Pablo gave Tomas drawings they had made of the compass. Esperanza wiped her eyes. "I wish you could stay longer," she told Tomas. "But the compass is telling you to go home, isn't it?" Tomas nodded. The crystal face was starting to shimmer again, just like in the attic. "I'll never forget you," he promised. "Or you," she said. Miguel pressed a small rolled map into Tomas's hands. "So you'll remember our adventure." As the compass began to glow brighter, Tomas felt the same warm, spinning sensation as before. "Goodbye!" he called as the world blurred around him. When everything cleared, he was back in his grandmother's attic, holding the compass and Miguel's map. But now he understood that some treasures are meant to be shared, and the greatest adventures happen when you help others.

illustrations.description: A group of diverse families walks up a mountain path at sunset, led by three children. The valley below shows a small refugee camp with tents and cooking fires. Golden light bathes the scene while the magical compass glows softly in the boy's hands. Everyone looks hopeful despite their journey's hardships.
illustrations.alt: The group leading refugees to safety in the mountains

illustrations.description: A young boy sits in a dusty attic holding both a glowing compass and an old rolled map. Warm sunlight streams through the window, and his face shows wonder and satisfaction. The magical shimmer around him suggests he's just returned from an incredible adventure.
illustrations.alt: Tomas back in the attic after his adventure

metadata.illustrationStyle: Warm, inviting adventure illustration style. Earthy tones, golden light. Detailed environments with hidden details kids can discover.

questions.question: Where does Tomas find the magical compass?
questions.explanation: Tomas discovers the magical compass in a wooden box while searching through his grandmother's dusty attic.

questions.question: What year does Tomas travel to when he uses the compass?
questions.explanation: Esperanza tells Tomas that he has traveled to Granada in the year 1492.

questions.question: What is Miguel's job in the story?
questions.explanation: Miguel works as a mapmaker's apprentice and knows all the secret paths and tunnels in Granada.

questions.question: What are the names of Carmen's children?
questions.explanation: Carmen's children are Elena, who is seven years old, and Pablo, who is only five.

questions.question: How do the friends trick the guards into leaving?
questions.explanation: Tomas writes a fake message saying the prisoners escaped through the south gate, which makes the guards rush away.

questions.question: What does the compass help Tomas find throughout his adventure?
questions.explanation: The magical compass points toward people who need help, guiding Tomas to assist families escaping persecution.

questions.question: What does Miguel give Tomas before he returns home?
questions.explanation: Miguel gives Tomas a small rolled map as a keepsake to remember their adventure together.

pages.title: Complete the Adventure
pages.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete the story summary!
pages.text: Tomas found a magical _____ in his grandmother's attic. It transported him to Granada in the year _____. There he met _____, a brave girl whose family was in danger. Together with _____, a young mapmaker's apprentice, they helped many _____ escape to safety in the mountains.

pages.title: Character Connections
pages.instructions: Match each character with what they did to help in the adventure!
pages.text: _____ discovered the magical compass that started the adventure. _____ knew all the secret tunnels under the city. _____ climbed up to get the keys and rescue the children. _____ were the two children who got rescued from the prison.
